text.title: Spatial Computing Overview Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns in platform-selection decisions for spatial products. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Decision cites Pico install base from 2023 or Magic Leap 2 ecosystem optimism; reality in 2026 makes the platform a dead end.
antipattern.root-cause: Author reused older XR landscape doc; LLM trained on pre-2024 data without grounding.
antipattern.fix: Require 2025+ citations per platform row. Re-pull IDC + vendor 10-K + store policy docs before scoring.

antipattern.symptom: Decision-record recommends Vision Pro for an active-gaming consumer audience because the founder owns one.
antipattern.root-cause: Author let personal-hardware bias override audience + content-type fit.
antipattern.fix: Require red-team review by a second agent or reviewer. Force `audience_fit` + `content_type_fit` scores to dominate when they diverge from `install_base`.

antipattern.symptom: Launches on a single platform; vendor changes store fee or exits market; product cannot pivot.
antipattern.root-cause: Recommendation omitted fallback; risk note absent.
antipattern.fix: Always name a fallback (WebXR or porting plan); enumerate risks if fallback is weak.

antipattern.symptom: Cinematic-video product launched on Quest 3 with hand-tracking-first interaction; users complain about poor passive viewing UX.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimized for install base only; ignored content-type fit.
antipattern.fix: Enforce content_type_fit score in the matrix. Mismatches must show in `risk_notes`.
